marketState: neutral, cross-currents: bearish technicals/storage vs bullish weather trend & crowded short positioning
summary: Front NG trades at $2.861, essentially at fair value (spread only -$0.06 to -$0.16 across the 0-21D fair curve, all inside the $2.42-$3.53 band) but well below its typical seasonal path (-9.1pp vs median, YTD -16.0% vs -6.9% median). Weather demand is running well above normal and building further out (1-7D +6.5% vs 5y, 8-16D +20.5% vs 5y at the top of its range), a bullish medium-term demand signal, while storage sits +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf surprise build, confirming an oversupplied backdrop. Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years, a crowded positioning extreme that raises squeeze risk on any bullish catalyst. Technicals have turned uniformly bearish from 15m through 1w (50<200 EMA, mostly negative MACD), arguing against a near-term long, and seasonality's own forward-looking odds are weak and unreliable (38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks, Low reliability, 59pp spread). Net: technicals favor the short-term short side, while the 3-12 week picture skews cautiously long on weather/positioning but stays capped by the storage glut.
